title: Pricing Product Manager
rationale: V9-1 acceptance: Vercel Pricing PM JD says role is open to (SF, NY, London, or Berlin). V8 dropped on non_na_territory because SF/NY were not tokenized as NA. V9 expands NA_CITIES_RE with bare US-city abbrev guards so SF/NY count as NA, producing tie → UNKNOWN → no drop.

revised_in.reason: V9-1 NA_CITIES_RE expansion (bare US-city abbrev guards SF/NY/LA/DC) fixes multi-region tie that V8 misclassified as NON_NA. Round 5 confirmed FP.

title: Software Engineer, AI SDK

title: Software Engineer - AI Systems
rationale: V9-1 acceptance: XBOW SE AI Systems JD has Location: Europe (Remote); US East Coast. V8 dropped on non_na_territory because US East Coast was not tokenized as NA. V9 adds NA_COAST_RE so coast descriptors register as NA — anchor tie + V9-2 location-section anchor → NA. Round 5 confirmed FP.

revised_in.reason: V9-1 NA_COAST_RE addition (US East Coast / US West Coast / coastal US tokens) fixes coast-descriptor coverage gap. Round 5 confirmed FP.

title: Forward Deployed Engineer, Infrastructure Specialist
rationale:
V9-2 acceptance: Cohere FDE Infrastructure Specialist JD has ## Location
Japan; Korea; Singapore markdown block. V8 left as UNKNOWN because parseJdSections strips the heading and the bare body Japan; Korea; Singapore did not match any ROLE_ANCHOR_PATTERN. Round 5 named cohort miss. V9-2 promotes location-typed section bodies (≤120 chars) to implicit anchor evidence; Japan/Korea/Singapore tokens → NON_NA anchor majority → NON_NA → drops on non_na_territory.

revised_in.reason: V9-2 markdown ## Location block matcher + location-typed section implicit anchors recognize Japan/Korea/Singapore as role-base evidence. Round 5 named cohort recovery (Will explicitly named Cohere Singapore as should-drop).

title: Engineering Manager, AI Engineering:Workflow Catalog
rationale: V10-1 acceptance: GitLab Engineering Manager Workflow Catalog JD has location line "Remote, EMEA; Remote, US-Southeast" parsed as a location-typed section. V9-2 promoted the EMEA NON_NA token via numeric majority (US-Southeast did not tokenize through anchor regex due to symmetric-delimiter requirement) so it wrongly hard-dropped on non_na_territory. V10-1 gates the implicit anchor on body-level NA/NON_NA token presence: NA=1 (US-) + NON_NA=1 (EMEA) → NA token present and NON_NA not strictly-majority (>2x) → suppress implicit anchor entirely → no anchor fires from this section → body-tie default UNKNOWN → no hard-drop. Round 6 confirmed FP — role explicitly accepts US-Southeast remote (Will-eligible).

revised_in.reason: V9-2 implicit location-section anchor promoted EMEA NON_NA token via numeric majority while body NA token (US-Southeast) was not tokenized through anchor regex. Round 6 confirmed FP — role explicitly accepts US-Southeast remote.
V10-1 symmetric gate suppresses implicit NON_NA anchor when NA tokens present and NON_NA not strictly dominant (>2x). No anchor fires from this section → body-tie default UNKNOWN → no hard-drop.

title: Forward Deployed Engineer - Software Engineer
rationale:
V10-1 acceptance: ElevenLabs FDE Software Engineer JD has "## Location
San Francisco; Brazil; France; India; New York" markdown block. V9-2 promoted Brazil/France/India NON_NA tokens via simple-majority (3 > 2) despite SF + NY being explicit NA bases. V10-1 gate: body NA=2 (SF, NY) + NON_NA=3 (Brazil, France, India). 3 NOT > 2*2 → fall through → suppress implicit anchor entirely → body-tie default UNKNOWN → no hard-drop. Round 6 confirmed FP — role explicitly lists San Francisco and New York as accepted bases (Will-eligible).

revised_in.reason: V9-2 implicit location-section anchor wrongly promoted Brazil/France/India NON_NA via simple-majority (3 > 2 SF/NY) without considering that SF and NY are explicit NA bases. Round 6 confirmed FP.
V10-1 symmetric gate: NA tokens present + NON_NA not strictly dominant (3 NOT > 4 = 2*2) → suppress implicit anchor entirely → body-tie default UNKNOWN → no hard-drop.
